draft.introduction: Surah Ar-Rahman ("The Most Merciful") is distinguished by its refrain, repeated thirty-one times: "Which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?" The surah catalogs the signs of divine mercy in creation and the afterlife.

translation.text: The Most Merciful—He taught the Quran. He created the human being. He taught him eloquent speech. The sun and the moon move by precise calculation. The stars and the trees prostrate. And the sky He raised, and He established the balance, that you may not transgress the balance. Establish weight in justice and do not fall short in the balance. And the earth He laid out for living creatures. In it are fruit and palm trees with sheaths, and grain with husks and fragrant plants. So which of the favors of your Lord will you both deny?
translation.original_text: الرَّحْمَـنُ عَلَّمَ الْقُرْآنَ خَلَقَ الْإِنسَانَ عَلَّمَهُ الْبَيَانَ

translator_notes.note: The refrain fa-bi-ayyi ala'i rabbikuma tukadhdhibani addresses both humans and jinn (the dual form -kuma), reflecting the Quranic cosmology in which both species are held accountable. The rhetorical force lies in the accumulation: after each new sign of divine generosity, the question becomes harder to answer.
